label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's break down this question about causation. The question is: Did Janet not putting oil in the machine cause it to break down? Let me understand the scenario: - Janet is responsible for putting oil in the machines. - Janet knows how to grease and oil machines. - Kate is in HR, also knows how to grease and oil machines. - If Janet doesn't put oil in the machines, it is not Kate's responsibility to do so. - One day, Janet forgot to put oil in an important machine. - Janet noticed she forgot but did not do anything. - Kate didn't notice Janet's failure and also didn't put oil in the machine. - The machine broke down a few days later. - The question is about whether Janet's failure to put oil in the machine caused the breakdown. Let me analyze the causal chain: - Machines need oil to function properly. - The machine wasn't oiled. - The lack of oil led to the machine breaking down. - Janet was responsible
